titleStmt.title: SII 27.165: original edition by M. D. Sampath, H. K. Narasimhamurthy, Madhav N. Katti and B. R. Gopal
No. 165. (A.R. No. 356 of 1930-31.) HEMMĀḌI, COONDAPOOR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.

summary.p: Slab set up in front of the Lakshmīnārāyaṇadēva temple.
This badly damaged record is dated Śaka 1440 (1441 current) corresponding to 1518-19 A.D., all other details being lost.

It seems to register a gift of the village Hemmāḍi and also money income from out of taxes to god [Raghu]nātha for offerings, probably by Vijayappa-oḍeya under the orders of the king. Mallarasa, the karaṇika of Bārakūru wrote the record.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śubhamastu śrī gaṇādhipatayēṁ namaḥ . namastuṁga siṟaścuṁbi caṁdracāma
ra cāravē . trayilōkya nagarāraṁbha mūlastaṁbhāya śaṁbhavē . svastiśrī jayābhyudaya śālivā
hana śakavaruṣa 1440 saṁdu varttamāna voṁdaneya
worn out
stige sahavāgi dhāreyaneṟadu koṭṭasarva nemū
rastaḷadaheṁmāḍiya grāmanu nimage sarvvamānyavāgi
grāmanu Ā caṁdrākkastāyigaḷāgi sarvamānyavāgi Anubhavisi baha
kruṣṇadēvarāyamahārāyara nirūpadiṁda vijayappa Oḍeyaru Ā vi dalu
Oḍeyaru śrī nātha dēvara Amrutapaḍige sarvamānyavāgi
parāga puṁṇyakāladalu sahiraṁṇyōdaka dāna dhārāpūrvakavāgi dhāreneṟadu barasikoṭa dharma
śilāśāsanakrama heṁmāḍiya grāmada catussīmeyavivarā . baḍagalu hariva hoḷeyiṁ teṁkalu
mūḍalu nāḍagaḍiyiṁ paḍavalu teṁkalu harivahoḷeyiṁ baḍagalu paḍuvalu
mūḍalu karināḍa kudurusaha yiṁtī catussīme Oḷagaṇa kuḷakāla baṁdake kāṭi ga 500 ke ghaṭi varaha
ga 200 Akṣāradalu yiṁnūṟu varahanū raghunāta dēvarige heṁmāḍi Uru dēśadavara te
barasi koṭa śilāśāsana bārakūra karaṇika mallarasara baraha vijayapa Oḍeya
ra Oppa śrīvittarāga heṁmāḍiya Uru dēśadavara Oppa . dānapālanaryyōmadhyē dānāśrēyō
nrupālanaṁ . dānāsargamavāpnōti pālanādacutaṁ padaṁ . svadatā -paradatāṁ vā yyō harētuvasu
ṁdhara . śaṣṭirvaruśa sahasrāṇi viṣṭāyāṁ jāyatē krimi . maṁgaḷa śrī śrī śrī śrī
Digital edition of SII 27.165 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
